mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. The signal is always active because consumer spending drives a large portion of US economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of GDP at decision time
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of RSAFS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is negative or less than 0.03, or t-test of mean returns in RSAFS-up months vs down months is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25%, it signals the start of monetary tightening, increasing the discount rate on equity cash flows and reducing valuations, producing negative SPX returns over the next quarter. At the decision time, FEDFUNDS is at 0.13%, so this mechanism is off.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS > 0.25, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and decreases in unemployment (UNRATE) signal broad economic strength from both construction and labor markets, jointly indicating a robust expansion that drives equity prices higher over the following month. The interaction between housing and labor is more informative than either alone.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal (e.g., factor combining HOUST growth and UNRATE decrease) over 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 in any 3-year window, or the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-2009 economic recovery regime, growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects sustained expansionary momentum, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive SPX returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is regime-conditional because GDP growth relates differently during recessions.
failure_condition: During the post-2009 period, the 60-day forward return following above-trend GDP growth is negative more often than positive, or Rank IC of GDP growth and returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.5%, it signals labor market weakness leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, predicting negative short-term SPX returns over the next week. At snapshot, UNRATE is 8.1%, below threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has been declining from peak of 10% in 2009
failure_condition: In episodes where UNRATE > 8.5%, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate demand-pull inflation that is not yet met with tightening, which supports equity valuations as nominal revenues grow. The interaction between inflation and low rates amplifies the positive signal for near-term SPX returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with low inflation
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, the 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive, or the interaction coefficient in a predictive regression is not positive and significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing expansion regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing factory output and investment, which leads to higher corporate profits and positive SPX returns over the following month. The mechanism is regime-conditional: only active when manufacturing is expanding (INDPRO above its 12-month moving average).
failure_condition: During manufacturing expansion periods, the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive, or the Rank IC of INDPRO growth (relative to trend) is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding employment and income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, always providing a positive signal for SPX returns over the next month. The trend in payrolls is a reliable indicator of economic momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is negative or insignificant, or mean returns in periods of rising payrolls are not higher than in falling payrolls at 10% significance.
